🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-18 05:36 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?Not yet — 1x 🚨 unchanged (INC-8064 H2Bet duplicate bonuses, now 53+ days static, REW-176 still parked to mid-Aug, all three breaches unchanged) plus 2x 🔴 unchanged and getting worse overnight (INC-14476 Mystake: True P1, partner silence now ~19h since the root-cause ask — SOC's 07:05 CEST handover confirms 'no feedback from partner as of yet', incident.io record still unrefreshed; INC-13895 Fusion API: partner silence now ~45h on the slug-confirmation ask, still no re-chase sent, incident now 4 days old). Otherwise a quiet night: zero incidents resolved, zero new Slack/Intercom activity across all 17 carried-over incidents, one new P2 (INC-14598 ElastiCache memory alert) opened and promptly worked this morning with no breach. Morning SOC handover (B8) checked clean — all 3 flagged items are tracked with an existing owner.
Urgent vs smaller1 urgent (🚨), 2 need a nudge (🔴), 10 watch-level (🟡), 5 well-handled (🟢)
Total open (active, excl. triage)18 +1 vs last digest (18 vs 17) -- 1 new, 0 resolved
1
🚨 Escalate now
2
🔴 Needs attention
10
🟡 Watch
5
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14598 — P2 ElastiCache memory-utilization alert on production-304-gen — Gérard acked within a minute, first working update within ~77min confirming instance already at max size (monitoring not upgrading), status moved to Fixing by 07:12 CEST; no breach, working normally

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • none

📋 Overnight handover

  • INC-14503 Meridianbet — no alerts triggered overnight, SRE to confirm if resolvable — tracked INC-14503, assigned to Giancarlo Laferla (existing Incident Lead) ✅
  • INC-14476 Mystake — partner recon-feed misconfig, no feedback from partner yet — tracked INC-14476, assigned to Joachim Farrugia (existing Incident Lead) ✅
  • INC-14598 ElastiCache memory alert — monitoring at 91%, upgrade only if necessary — tracked INC-14598, assigned to Gérard E. Pelayo (existing Incident Lead) ✅
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 1

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 52d· evidence to 09 Jul 09:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

All three breaches behind the 🚨 flag (A2/A4, B5, C10/D15) still hold with no change this cycle — Slack, Intercom, and incident.io are all exactly as static as last run, re-confirmed again on the 18 Jul morning read (zero new messages anywhere). This classification reflects the 16 Jul management note (H2Bet accepted the workaround; REW-176 opened 9 Jul is the correct read), but the note does not resolve the underlying breaches: REW-176 is still capacity-blocked until mid-Aug, BetConstruct has shown zero traction, and the incident.io record still does not self-containedly reflect the true state that only Intercom and the management note carry (D15). Now 53+ days open.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed as root cause within hours of the 26 May report, but 53 days later there is still no deployed fix — roughly 300 duplicate grants/week continue. Per the management note, H2Bet has accepted the interim workaround and the resulting ticket REW-176 was opened 9 Jul, but it remains parked on Rewards-team capacity until mid-August. This cycle showed zero new activity anywhere: no new Slack messages, no new Intercom parts, no new incident.io status-update text.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: still holds — 53 days in, no decisive fix deployed, Urgent ticket (REW-176) still parked with no queue movement this cycle
  • B5: still holds — zero evidence of any BetConstruct response or outreach progress since the 18 Jun follow-up note
  • C10/D15: still holds — Intercom conversation remains closed (since 06 Jul) while incident.io stays open P1 with no incident.io-side explanation of the closure or REW-176 status; the explanation exists only in Andrea's management note, not in the incident record itself
Proposed / pending
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note), opened Urgent 9 Jul, still blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug — no movement observed this cycle
Timeline (4)
  • No new Slack messages in the incident channel since last read (pagination exhausted, zero results)
  • No new incident.io update text since 16 Jul 20:30 UTC
  • Intercom conversation unchanged: still closed, last part remains 06 Jul 08:11:48Z (Oracle CSAT auto-request) following Andrea's own 06 Jul close-out comment
  • No new activity confirming or updating REW-176's Rewards-queue status this cycle

→ Decide this week: pull REW-176 forward in Rewards' queue given ~300 duplicate grants/week of continuing leakage, or formally document risk-acceptance until mid-Aug; separately escalate the BetConstruct account-manager/root-cause gap to leadership, and add an incident.io status update reflecting the management note's true state so the record is self-contained per D15.

🔴 Needs attention 2

INC-14476 slack intercomP1open 20h· evidence to 17 Jul 18:05 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Mystake Production

True P1, 'Waiting on Partner' for ~19h+ as of the 18 Jul morning run (no new Slack/Intercom activity overnight; SOC handover explicitly confirms the partner is still silent), incident.io record still doesn't reflect the applied mitigation or current owner (D15). Handling itself is competent (precise root cause, proactive internal workaround instead of just waiting) but partner silence approaching a full day on a True P1 now warrants an active chase.

Cocobet (Mystake) blocked users appear to 'unblock' themselves because the partner's daily recon feed omits users who should stay blocked. Joachim confirmed the exact mechanism and sent the partner a clear technical ask early on (12:17-12:19 CEST); the partner then went silent all day — confirmed unresponsive by Giancarlo at ~19:07 CEST. Rather than keep waiting, Giancarlo applied an internal mitigation (~19:09-19:47 CEST): resynced Cocobet's origin and blacklisted it from the daily recon job (~20k players on that origin), then handed the incident to Matteo Rapisarda at shift change. Status remains 'Waiting on Partner' with zero partner response since the initial ask; the incident.io record's own update text still doesn't mention the mitigation or handover.

Open breaches
  • C9/C10: partner has not replied since being given the root-cause explanation/ask at 12:18 CEST 17 Jul — silence now confirmed overnight via the 07:05 CEST 18 Jul SOC handover ('no feedback from partner as of yet'), ~19h after the ask; for a True P1, this now warrants an active chase (account/partner manager), not further passive waiting
  • D15: incident.io record has not been refreshed since the 10:31 UTC update — it still omits the internal mitigation applied ~19:09-19:47 CEST (origin resync + recon blacklist) and the shift handover to Matteo Rapisarda, now going on ~19h stale
Proposed / pending
  • Resync affected origin via user migration and blacklist Cocobet's origin from the daily recon job — applied and confirmed by Giancarlo ~19:47 CEST 17 Jul (~20k players on that origin)
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-17T08:42
  • root-cause explanation and recon-feed check request — delivered 12:18-12:19 CEST; no partner reply since, on Intercom or Slack, through at least 20:05 CEST 17 Jul
Timeline (6)
  • 17 Jul 10:17 UTC (12:17 CEST) Joachim confirms exact mechanism with 2 specific blocked users and sends partner a technical ask
  • 17 Jul 13:34 CEST Joachim: 'No feedback as of yet from the partner on this'
  • 17 Jul 19:06-19:08 CEST Andrea asks 'any traction on this?'; Giancarlo confirms partner unresponsive since ~14:00 CEST re-ping, likely signed off
  • 17 Jul 19:09-19:47 CEST Giancarlo runs a user migration to resync Cocobet's origin and blacklists it from daily recon; confirms done ~19:47 CEST, ~20k players on that origin
  • 17 Jul 19:10 CEST handover to Matteo Rapisarda for the 19:30 CEST shift change
  • 17 Jul 20:05 CEST incident.io bot prompts for a status update — none posted since; incident.io record unchanged since the 10:31 UTC update

→ Have Joachim (Incident Lead) or the current owner (Matteo Rapisarda) escalate to Cocobet's account/partner manager to force a response on fixing the recon feed, and post an incident.io update capturing the applied mitigation (origin resync + recon blacklist) and current owner so the record is self-contained.

INC-13895 slackP2open 3d· evidence to 17 Jul 08:32 UTC
[FIRING:1] Fusion API errors Alerting (spinbet-terragon: 401 @ /external/pusher/:brand true)

P2, open since 14 Jul (4+ days), no lead activity or incident.io update since 17 Jul — confirmed zero new Slack/incident.io activity again on the 18 Jul morning read; partner has been silent on a direct, correct technical ask for ~45h with no re-chase attempted — passive waiting per D12, and this is the same recurrence pattern that the recently-closed INC-12617 was flagged (A4) for not addressing long-term.

Fusion API 401 errors for Spinbet Terragon are blocking on-site notifications and Rich Inbox messages, root-caused to a brand-slug mismatch (partner sends 'spinbetterragon', FT accepts 'spinbet-terragon'/'vegastars'). A correct technical ask for the partner to confirm the definitive slug was sent 16 Jul 10:29 CEST. Zero new Slack activity and no new incident.io status update since the last digest cycle — the partner has still not replied, and no re-chase has been sent.

Open breaches
  • C10/D12: partner has not responded to the 16 Jul slug-confirmation ask for ~45h and no re-chase has been sent; incident now 4+ days old (since 14 Jul) with player notifications still blocked for Spinbet Terragon
Proposed / pending
  • Confirm definitive brand slug with partner before changing FT config - still pending, unacknowledged
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • accurate technical explanation + slug-confirmation ask sent 16 Jul 10:29 CEST
Timeline (3)
  • No new Slack messages or incident.io updates since 17 Jul 08:32 UTC read
  • Status remains 'Waiting on Partner' since 14 Jul 12:34 UTC; no update since
  • Partner slug-confirmation ask outstanding since 16 Jul 10:29 CEST (~2 days) with no re-chase sent

→ Have Incident Lead Joachim Farrugia send a direct re-chase to the partner today referencing the 16 Jul ask; if still no reply within 24h, escalate via the partner's account manager rather than continuing to wait silently.

🟡 Watch 10

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open 15d· evidence to 17 Jul 13:55 UTC
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: Solved as an incident. Only pending item is with Estelarbet, who need to identify which players were impacted — this is being checked and shared from our side; we need their input on which players complained so we can cross-check. Confirmed via the partner Intercom ticket.

Per management note: solved as an incident, only the partner cross-check remains open — active work continuing (Giancarlo checked again 17 Jul, partner added a clarifying comment that evening), not stalled. No new Slack/Intercom activity confirmed this run. Held at 🟡 pending FT's reply to the partner's scope clarification, now due today.

Cloudflare-origin check blocked shortlink/unsubscribe traffic; mitigated same day, fix merged 02 Jul. Per Andrea's management note this is solved as an incident, pending only Estelarbet's cross-check of impacted players. Giancarlo's cross-check on 17 Jul 11:44 UTC came back inconclusive; later that evening (22:35 UTC) the partner (Gloria Morocho) added a clarifying comment on Intercom — the reported error affected ALL urls (not just the unsubscribe link), across both notifications and emails, which reframes the scope of the cross-check. No FT reply to that clarification yet as of this run (~3.5h later); the Intercom conversation is now waiting on FT's response (state flipped to waiting-on-us at 22:35:55Z). No new Slack activity this cycle.

Open breaches
  • C10: impact cross-check still inconclusive, final report to partner not yet delivered; partner's 22:35 UTC clarification (all urls affected, not just unsubscribe) remains unanswered as of this 18 Jul morning run (~7h now, no longer just overnight) — worth a same-day reply
Proposed / pending
  • CF-origin carve-out - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - cross-check inconclusive so far, now needs to incorporate the partner's broader-scope clarification
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • last partner message unanswered
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly (13-15 Jul), still not fully delivered
Timeline (4)
  • 17 Jul 08:07 UTC Giancarlo starts cross-check on partner-supplied complainant ID
  • 17 Jul 11:44 UTC Giancarlo reports cross-check inconclusive; a different user's unsubscribe worked fine
  • 17 Jul 13:55 CEST status Monitoring → Waiting on Partner
  • 17 Jul 22:35 UTC partner (Gloria Morocho) clarifies on Intercom: the redirect affected all urls, not only unsubscribe, across notifications and emails — no FT reply yet

→ Reply to the partner's 22:35 UTC clarification (all urls affected, not just unsubscribe) and fold it into the cross-check/report Estelarbet is waiting on.

INC-14565 intercomP1open 9h· evidence to 17 Jul 20:57 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Segurobet Production

Strong handling mechanics (immediate ack, partner response within P1 SLA, root cause fixed, partner confirmed satisfied), but incident.io wasn't updated after partner confirmation (C10/D15) and this is the second same-day recurrence of a capacity issue with no confirmed durable fix yet (A4).

Segurobet partner contact Danilo Tostes reported via Intercom (bot-classified P1) that SMS sends for CRM Activity 3186 weren't firing; a clone attempt (3192) also failed. Root cause was the Segurobet 'am-fire' notification instance having been downsized (an EV from 8 Jul) combined with a misconfigured prefetch count that kept the KEDA scaledobject from scaling — the same failure mode recurred earlier that same day at 05:19 CEST. Matteo Rapisarda (Incident Lead) diagnosed and fixed it directly with the partner in Intercom, fully drained both queues, and the partner confirmed satisfaction less than 90 minutes after reporting.

Open breaches
  • C10/D15: Partner confirmed resolution in Intercom at 21:35 UTC, but the incident.io record (status still 'Waiting on Partner') was never touched afterward to reflect that the loop is closed
  • A4: second same-day recurrence of a capacity issue traced to an 8-Jul EV — mitigated manually again rather than a confirmed durable/tracked fix
Proposed / pending
  • Upgrade am-fire instance to 4xlarge, fix replicas at 20, reduce prefetch/batch size — applied and effective
  • Persist the corrected capacity settings so the 8-Jul downgrade EV doesn't cause a repeat — discussed live between Matteo and Andrea, outcome not yet visible
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-17T20:06
  • Activity 3192 blacklisted so no further SMS sent from it — delivered ~20:46Z
  • Backlog would clear shortly — delivered, confirmed fully drained by 20:57Z
  • Asked Segurobet to verify queries/tickets working as expected — partner confirmed 'Tudo certo' at 21:35Z
Timeline (6)
  • 17 Jul 20:06 UTC Danilo Tostes opens Intercom ticket re: SMS not sending on Activity 3186/3192; bot auto-classifies P1; incident.io auto-creates INC-14565
  • 20:39 UTC (~29 min after ticket open, within Oracle's 30-min P1 SLA) Matteo replies to Danilo, applies mitigation
  • Matteo traces root cause to an 8-Jul EV (Agustín Castaño) that downsized the am-fire instance; same issue hit earlier that day at 05:19 CEST
  • 20:57 UTC am-fire and SMS queues fully drained; status → Waiting on Partner
  • 21:35 UTC Danilo replies 'Tudo certo! Obrigado' — partner confirms resolution
  • 22:41 CEST two related sub-incidents (INC-14573, INC-14575) merged into INC-14565

→ Post an incident.io update reflecting partner confirmation and formally close; confirm the capacity settings are persisted so the 8-Jul EV pattern doesn't recur a third time.

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 29d· evidence to 17 Jul 09:55 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

Continued thorough, collaborative technical investigation (Matteo/Stephen) into event-ordering reliability, now with Integration/PM (Robert) engaged and a scoping ask outstanding since 17 Jul 10:22 CEST — no breach, genuinely still resolving a data-modeling/ownership question before deciding. The gap since Friday afternoon is a weekend lull, not a stall.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags. Andrea sent Matteo's drafted handover to Integration/PM (Robert Friberg, Lis, Gui Furst) on 17 Jul, framing it as needing a permanent dev fix. Robert pushed back that GiG should fix their own event ordering rather than FT building custom logic, but asked SRE/Integration to scope how big a change the timestamp-based (update_epoch) fix would be as a fallback. No new Slack or Intercom activity since Stephen/Matteo's joint analysis concluded Friday 17 Jul 11:55 CEST — a quiet Saturday, not a stall.

Proposed / pending
  • historical re-sync - done multiple rounds
  • update_epoch as ordering key - drafted handover identifies this as a candidate fix; Robert asked Stephen/Curt to scope size of this fallback vs pushing GiG to fix event ordering themselves; scoping answer still pending as of 18 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • structural handover to Integration/PM drafted 16 Jul, sent to Robert/Lis/Gui 17 Jul
Timeline (6)
  • 16 Jul 10:03 CEST Matteo posts a fully-drafted partner message and an internal SRE→Integration/PM handover memo (root cause, options, open decisions), asks Andrea for sign-off
  • 16 Jul 14:58-15:13 CEST Matteo tells partner (Anna) a correction pass for accounts affected since 30 Jun is coming, and that he'll regroup internally with Lis/Integration on the long-term fix -- partner satisfied for now ('sounds great, thanks')
  • 17 Jul 07:58 CEST Andrea sends the full SRE->Integration/PM handover to Robert Friberg, Lis, and Gui Furst -- root cause, options, and the ask, as drafted by Matteo yesterday
  • 17 Jul 10:22 CEST Robert Friberg: prefers pushing GiG to fix event ordering on their end rather than FT building custom logic, but asks Stephen/Curt to scope the size of the update_epoch-based fallback fix and whether FT has done this before elsewhere
  • 17 Jul 10:57-11:55 CEST: Stephen and Matteo continue rigorous joint analysis of GiG update_epoch reliability for reordering assign/unassign events; conclusion reached that update_epoch (not entry_timestamp) is reliable for ordering since opposite-polarity events for the same tag are never within the same second, despite ~30% of events losing sub-second precision
  • No further Slack messages or Intercom parts as of 18 Jul (Saturday) — Stephen/Curt's scoping answer to Robert not yet posted

→ Await Stephen/Curt's scoping answer to Robert (outstanding since 17 Jul), then get a final decision on GiG escalation vs. FT-side timestamp fix.

INC-11079 slackP2open 20d· evidence to 16 Jul 10:54 UTC
segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager (messages: 1, in: 0/s)
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: this is in reality a BetConstruct platform issue (their Operator API returns 500 on consent updates). The partner is aware and has raised it with BetConstruct themselves; active partner correspondence (examples provided, regular chasing incl. by Andrea) lives in the partner channel thread: https://fasttrack-solutions.slack.com/archives/C08VB1RBJLF/p1782549375502499 — future runs should treat that thread as the live track, not the quiet incident channel.

Per management note (16 Jul): BetConstruct-side root cause, active correspondence in partner thread, blocked on vendor not FT inaction — classification held at 🟡 since no new evidence contradicts the note; re-read the partner thread this cycle and confirmed BetConstruct still has not given an ETA and Andrea's latest chase (16 Jul) remains unanswered.

Segurobet Consent Manager remains stalled since 27 Jun on partner (BetConstruct) Operator API 500s. Per management note, the live track is the partner thread, not this incident channel. The main incident channel had no new activity this cycle. In the partner thread, Andrea re-chased BetConstruct's Eduardo Nunes on 16 Jul but as of this read that message has received no reply — still no ETA from BetConstruct.

Open breaches
  • External blocker: BetConstruct 'at a loss' — no ETA; Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase unanswered, confirmed still the last message in the partner thread as of this 18 Jul read (2 days now)
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-27T08:36
  • last partner message unanswered
  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given; Andrea re-chased 16 Jul and that re-chase remains unanswered
Timeline (2)
  • No new activity in the main incident channel this cycle
  • 16 Jul 12:54 CEST: Andrea re-chased Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) in the partner thread for an update — still unanswered as of this read

→ Consider an FT-side mitigation (locally suppress sends for failed opt-outs) to remove compliance exposure while waiting; consider a further partner escalation if the 16 Jul chase remains unanswered much longer.

INC-14346 slackP2open 41h· evidence to 17 Jul 21:42 UTC
CF Threat Watch: brute-force-suspect,login-success-after-fails on ft-crm.com (41.76.35.208)

Handling itself was fast and correct (B6 escalation, false-positive determination), but the record has now gone unclosed long enough that incident.io's own bot flagged it for resolution on 17 Jul 23:42 CEST and nobody responded — confirmed still untouched on the 18 Jul morning read (no new Slack activity) — a one-click closure sitting idle is a minor D15 record-hygiene gap worth a nudge to Stephen, not a live incident concern.

CF Threat Watch brute-force-suspect alert on ft-crm.com. Correct B6 security escalation flow despite paging misfires; team read is a shared-network false positive per Cloudflare's own classification. Status remains 'Investigating' with no incident.io updates since 2026-07-16T12:40:33Z, and incident.io's own auto-nudge on 17 Jul asking to mark it resolved has gone unanswered.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident sitting in 'Investigating' ~43h+ with the conclusion settled and even the automated resolve-nudge left unactioned — record not formally closed despite no remaining work
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 12:10 — alert fires, incident opened, escalated to P2 Investigating within minutes
  • 16 Jul 12:40 — last incident.io status update; team settles on shared-network false-positive read
  • 17 Jul 23:42 CEST — incident.io bot auto-prompts to mark resolved — no reply, no action taken

→ Stephen Riolo to click resolve/close now that the false-positive conclusion is settled.

INC-14503 slackP2open 16h· evidence to 17 Jul 14:40 UTC
Meridianbet Migration and Sendouts Alerts

Fast, competent scaling response containing a wide multi-service alert storm, but the record doesn't yet explain what actually caused the migration/sendout backlog. 18 Jul run: a 01:42 CEST bot nudge ('no activity, can this be resolved?') went unanswered overnight, and the 07:05 CEST SOC handover flags it again ('no alerts triggered during night shift, to be confirmed by SRE if this incident could be resolved') — quiet and stable, just needs someone to confirm closure or add the root cause.

Meridianbet-wide delayed message processing across CRM integration, Singularity, kafka-shovel and RabbitMQ, absorbing 12+ merged alerts/incidents. Joachim took lead instantly, scaled up Singularity, and by 15:31 CEST 7 attached alerts had resolved; status moved to Monitoring. Lead handed to Giancarlo at 16:34 CEST as more alerts kept attaching and resolving through 16:40 CEST. Root cause itself is still recorded as 'Unknown' in incident.io despite the mitigation working.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io 'Causes' field still 'Unknown' despite substantial mitigation already applied
Proposed / pending
  • Scale up Singularity - applied, working
Timeline (4)
  • 17 Jul 12:53 UTC alert fires, 8 alerts attached within the hour, Joachim leads
  • 17 Jul 15:03 CEST Joachim: kafka lag on consumer now processing (after scaling Singularity)
  • 17 Jul 15:30 CEST Andrea updates status to Monitoring; 7 attached alerts resolved
  • 17 Jul 16:34 CEST lead handed to Giancarlo; more alerts still merging/resolving through 16:40 CEST

→ Whoever holds the lead (Giancarlo) to add the root cause once identified, not just the mitigation steps.

INC-10902 slackP3open 22d· evidence to 17 Jul 15:00 UTC
H2Bet Clickhouse Writer Service Investigation

A4 long-runner with fresh, concrete ownership as of 17 Jul (Andrea's reassignment) — no regression since, but the promised 'next week' commitment hasn't materialized into a date yet; only one day has passed and Andrea's own framing sets next week as the natural check-in point, so this stays 🟡 rather than escalating.

Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer queue pileup (consolidated bucket since 26 Jun, channel renamed 17 Jul to reflect the current investigation). On 17 Jul Andrea reassigned Incident Lead to Matteo Rapisarda (Simon Brown as backup) and said he's 'moving this to you next week', citing Matteo's recent Leonbets root-cause work. Since that reassignment, the only channel activity has been automated — no human message and no committed date for the schema/type-mismatch fix yet.

Open breaches
  • A4: root-cause payload type-mismatch bug identified 26 Jun, still unfixed 22 days later; new owner (Matteo) assigned 17 Jul but has not yet posted or committed to a fix date
Proposed / pending
  • Schema/type-mismatch fix (ActionEvent.Status int vs string) — still not implemented, ownership handed to Matteo as of 17 Jul
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 16:33-16:34 CEST Andrea reassigns lead Simon Brown → Matteo Rapisarda, Simon as backup, referencing Matteo's Leonbets work ('moving this to you next week')
  • 17 Jul 17:00 CEST channel renamed to 'H2Bet Clickhouse Writer Service Investigation' (was 'H2Bet incidents - night - DO NOT CLOSE')
  • 17 Jul 17:00-17:01 CEST 5 attached alerts auto-resolved — no further human commentary since the reassignment

→ Confirm Matteo has picked this up and get a concrete date for the schema/type-mismatch fix early next week, per Andrea's own timeline.

INC-12998 slackP3open 8d· evidence to 17 Jul 13:36 UTC
frank-casino: deployment ping-pong is stuck on CrashLoopBackOff
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: the deployment ping-pong service is not critical — a crash-loop here is not a critical-path failure (B7 does not apply). Watch-level, not escalate.

Watch-level per management note (ping-pong non-critical); Andrea remains personally engaged and is actively working the severity/status.

frank-casino ping-pong deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff (403 from integration API on ping). Andrea personally severity-adjusted P2→P3, briefly paused the incident until tomorrow morning then immediately resumed it to Investigating — continued personal engagement on a service Andrea has explicitly ruled non-critical (B7 doesn't apply).

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io summary field still blank; the 403 finding remains Slack-only
Timeline (1)
  • 17 Jul 15:35-15:36 CEST Andrea adjusts severity P2→P3, pauses then immediately resumes to Investigating

→ Giancarlo to post the 403-integration-API finding to incident.io.

INC-14284 slackP3open 2d· evidence to 17 Jul 19:50 UTC
Daily Data Science Message Spikes

Still a recurring pattern with no fix ETA (A4) — unchanged from last cycle's flag — but new evidence shows active management follow-up (Andrea reassigned the lead and posed direct accountability questions to Data Science on 17 Jul evening), not a silent drift; held at 🟡 pending Data Science's response.

Daily Data Science message spikes (heavy Singularity jobs querying the full player base ~7am/7pm) remain unresolved, confirmed no partner-facing impact, and the query-lightening fix still has no ETA. New this cycle: Andrea Azzopardi formally reassigned Incident Lead from Joachim Farrugia to Jeremy Curmi (17 Jul 21:50 CEST) and pressed Clayton Spiteri with pointed questions — what the Data Science service is, what depends on it, whether it can affect player experience via LC/Activities, and what SOC can actually do besides ignore the alarms — tasking Matteo Rapisarda and Giancarlo Laferla to get clarity 'next week'.

Open breaches
  • A4: recurring daily pattern persists with no committed fix date on the query-lightening work
  • A2/A4 (emerging): whether datascience slowness actually affects player experience via LC/Activities is now an open question Andrea raised herself — not yet answered by Data Science
Proposed / pending
  • Lighten Data Science queries — still no ETA, in backlog
  • Get clarity on service criticality/impact and SOC options from Data Science (Clayton Spiteri) — targeted for next week, not yet answered
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 20:15 CEST — bot nudge: no activity, can this be resolved? (correctly not closed)
  • 17 Jul 21:49 CEST — Andrea Azzopardi asks Clayton Spiteri to clarify Data Science's criticality/dependencies/player impact and what SOC can do; tags Matteo Rapisarda + Giancarlo Laferla for answers by next week
  • 17 Jul 21:50 CEST — Incident Lead formally reassigned from Joachim Farrugia to Jeremy Curmi

→ Chase Clayton Spiteri/Matteo Rapisarda/Giancarlo Laferla for the requested clarity (service criticality, player-experience impact, SOC options) and push for a firm ETA or backlog priority on lightening the queries, rather than letting 'next week' slip.

INC-14331 slackP3open 42h· evidence to 17 Jul 21:39 UTC
Leonbets Activity Manager Groups Alerts Spamming

Real progress (fix proven stable for 12h+) but no confirmation the durable merge has landed, and the incident is now drifting — two automated resolve-nudges unanswered — risking a silent close while the kubectl-level mitigation is still revert-exposed (A2).

Leonbets activity-manager DB-saturation alert spam; Matteo's scoped-loading fix (PR #490) proven effective over 12h+ (RDS CPU healthy ~40-50%, lag <2k), applied via kubectl-level changes that remain exposed to reverting on the next OneClick deploy until PR #490 is merged and the env vars made durable. Since the last digest, only a second automated bot check-in nudge has appeared in-channel asking if the incident can be resolved — again no human response, and no evidence PR #490 has actually merged.

Open breaches
  • A2: durable fix merge still unconfirmed/not verified merged; kubectl-level mitigation remains revert-exposed on next OneClick deploy. Two consecutive bot nudges (16:34 and 23:39 CEST 17 Jul) have gone unanswered, incident sitting in 'Monitoring' with no fresh human update in over 28 hours.
Proposed / pending
  • Scoped action loading (PR #490) - proven effective for 12h+, merge status still unconfirmed; durable persistence of env vars still pending
Timeline (2)
  • 17 Jul 16:34 CEST bot check-in nudge, unanswered
  • 17 Jul 23:39 CEST second bot check-in nudge ("no activity... mark as resolved?"), also unanswered

→ Ask Malcolm/reviewers to confirm and prioritize merging PR #490 now rather than 'next week', and have Matteo respond to the bot nudges so the incident isn't auto-resolved while the durable fix is still outstanding.

🟢 Under control 5

5x well-handled: INC-14598 (ElastiCache memory alert, fresh P2 promptly acked and worked this morning), INC-14423 (log4j CVE, Patrik personally deprioritized to next week with a tracked Linear ticket — a deliberate D12 call, not a silent close), INC-14351 (Stanleybet Romania false unsubscribe alarm, partner-agreed Monday fix test, snoozed accordingly), INC-13889 (Calendars orphan door variations, ownership gap closed and fix shipped, stable), INC-10810 (12play brands fire late, ownership gap closed, real fix already landed, stable)

INC-14351 slack intercomP2open 40h· evidence to 17 Jul 23:36 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Stanleybet Romania Production

No new Slack or Intercom human activity since the last run; the only new item is a routine automated staleness bot message, not a breach. Intercom conversation is snoozed until Monday 07:00 UTC, matching the jointly agreed plan (D12) — nothing contradicts the cached synthesis.

Email security scanners triggered false unsubscribe signals for Stanleybet Romania (no emails actually suppressed). Lead reassigned Joachim → Giancarlo; status moved Investigating → Fixing. Giancarlo and the partner (Marius Sindie) jointly agreed to test the fix Monday rather than risk a weekend change — Intercom conversation is now snoozed until Mon 20 Jul 07:00 UTC, consistent with that plan.

Proposed / pending
  • Fix in review (conversion-service-go PR #106), deploy/test Monday - agreed with partner
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-16T12:41
  • test fix Monday — agreed with partner
Timeline (4)
  • 17 Jul 15:07 CEST Giancarlo/partner confirm on Intercom: 'Agreed, we will re group Monday'
  • 17 Jul 15:11 CEST Giancarlo: 'To test fix Monday'
  • 17 Jul 15:47 CEST Andrea reassigns lead to Giancarlo, status → Fixing
  • 18 Jul 01:36 CEST automated incident.io bot nudge asking if incident can be resolved due to inactivity — no human response (expected on a weekend with no work due until Monday)
INC-14598 slackP2open 115m· evidence to 18 Jul 05:12 UTC
[FIRING:1] ElastiCache Memory Utilized alert Alerting production-304-gen (true p2 aws-elasticache)

Fresh P2 infra alert with prompt escalation-ack and lead assigned instantly; first working update landed within normal grace (~77min) and status is progressing (Triage→Fixing). Also flagged in the 07:05 CEST SOC handover with an existing owner. No breach evident — flagged for deep-read only because P2 severity always warrants a look.

ElastiCache memory-utilization alert for production-304-gen (aws-elasticache) fired at 03:44 UTC (05:44 CEST) 18 Jul; auto-escalation paged Gérard E. Pelayo, who acknowledged within a minute and was set as Incident Lead. First working update came ~77min later (07:01 CEST) confirming the instance is already at cache.r6g.4xlarge with memory holding ~90-91%, so monitoring rather than upgrading further. Joachim Farrugia then joined and ran a Redis key-cleaning pass on Galabet at 07:12 CEST, moving status Triage → Fixing.

Proposed / pending
  • Monitor at current instance size (cache.r6g.4xlarge); upgrade only if necessary — in progress
  • Redis key cleaning on Galabet — applied 07:12 CEST 18 Jul
Timeline (4)
  • 18 Jul 03:44 UTC alert fires, incident auto-created P2
  • 18 Jul 05:44 CEST Gérard E. Pelayo acknowledges, set as Incident Lead
  • 18 Jul 07:01 CEST Gérard reports memory holding ~90-91% on cache.r6g.4xlarge, monitoring rather than upgrading
  • 18 Jul 07:12 CEST Joachim Farrugia runs Redis key cleaning on Galabet; status moved Triage → Fixing
INC-10810 slackP3open 22d· evidence to 17 Jul 20:02 UTC
12play brands fire late

The previously open D15 breach (incident.io Incident Lead field showing Daniel Lindengren while Sam Lundborg was actually running the incident) is now closed — Andrea formally reassigned the Incident Lead role to Sam Lundborg on 17 Jul. No other guideline breach evident: the substantive fix already landed, status remains honestly 'Fixing' rather than being prematurely closed (D14), and this is a P3 judged leniently.

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload; a real fix landed (4 PRs, 2 DB upgrades) and status has held at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul while monitoring the fix and planning a RabbitMQ 4.x cutover with the partner. The previously flagged ownership-record gap is now resolved: on 17 Jul 22:02 CEST, Andrea Azzopardi formally set the Incident Lead to Sam Lundborg in incident.io, replacing Daniel Lindengren (on leave).

Proposed / pending
  • RDS/cluster upgrade maintenance window scheduled for 'next Wednesday' with the partner — outcome not confirmed in this cycle's evidence
  • RabbitMQ 4.x cutover coordination owned by Sam Lundborg — no update on scheduling/completion this cycle
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-24T16:00
  • update 12play via Intercom on fix progress - discussed internally, completion not confirmed (no new evidence this cycle)
Timeline (3)
  • 10 Jul 11:36 UTC — last substantive engineering update: key perf fixes merged (PR #1914, PR #2471), 12play RDS upgraded, next step is scheduling RabbitMQ 4.x cutover with Momentum
  • 17 Jul 22:02 CEST — Andrea Azzopardi formally reassigned Incident Lead from Daniel Lindengren to Sam Lundborg (only new Slack message and only incident.io change this cycle)
  • No status-update text since 10 Jul; status remains 'Fixing'
INC-14423 slackP3open 30h· evidence to 17 Jul 13:50 UTC
Container using an image vulnerable to CVE-2021-45046

D12 informed decision: Patrik (CTO) explicitly reviewed and deprioritized to next week with a tracked ticket — not a silent close, a deliberate call.

Log4j CVE-2021-45046 found on a staging container. Andrea reassigned lead to Stephen Riolo (security team) at 15:37 CEST; Patrik Potocki personally weighed in, directing that Agustín ack and a Linear ticket be opened so it can be tackled next week — an explicit, informed deprioritization by the CTO himself.

Proposed / pending
  • Upgrade log4j-core - in progress
  • Linear ticket for next-week fix - per Patrik's direction
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 23:30 UTC incident opened, staging-only Log4j CVE
  • 17 Jul 15:37 CEST Andrea reassigns lead Giancarlo → Stephen Riolo (security team)
  • 17 Jul 15:50 CEST Patrik: 'make sure Agustín acks and have a ticket on the linear board... can be tackled next week'
INC-13889 slack intercomP4open 3d· evidence to 17 Jul 20:01 UTC
Calendars: Orphan (but live) door variations in the database(!)

Previously flagged A1/D15 gap (no formally assigned Incident Lead) is resolved: Sam Lundborg is now the formal Incident Lead in incident.io, and the record is self-contained. P4, no player impact, fix shipped — no remaining breaches this cycle.

Door-only Chinese variation bug on 12play's Promotional Calendar 3 (no player impact) — root-caused to the backoffice editor reading only calendar-level variations. On 17 Jul 20:01 UTC, Andrea Azzopardi formally set Sam Lundborg as Incident Lead and moved status Investigating → Fixing, which auto-regenerated a clear, self-contained incident summary describing fixes shipped to rewards-backend and backoffice-v2.

Proposed / pending
  • backoffice-v2 PR — summary now states shipped
  • rewards-backend PR — summary now states shipped
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T04:15
  • fix confirmed tested, deploying Monday morning — current incident.io summary now describes the fix as shipped
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 20:01:29 UTC — Andrea Azzopardi set Incident Lead to Sam Lundborg (closing the prior ownership gap)
  • 17 Jul 20:01:35 UTC — status moved Investigating → Fixing; incident.io auto-regenerated summary describing fixes already shipped to rewards-backend and backoffice-v2
  • No further Slack activity in-channel since; no new partner messages
